street.thesis: ARM is the dominant ISA licensor for mobile and AI silicon; royalty rate uplift from v9/CSS migration and data center growth drive sustained 20%+ revenue growth through the decade

history.note: Non-GAAP EPS CAGR approximated from revenue/margin improvement since FY2024 IPO; revenue CAGR FY2023-FY2026 was 22.5% p.a.

peers.note: Closest royalty-model comp; QTL earns ~50% EBIT margin on patent royalties — ARM's closest structural peer, but QTL is mature and ARM is in a rate-upgrade cycle
IP/EDA peer; 30%+ FCF margins but growing at 15% not 25%; ARM deserves premium
IP/EDA peer; similar quality but slower growth trajectory
AI silicon reference point at mature scale; ARM at its royalty build-out is earlier stage

contested_lever: Royalty rate per chip (blended): v9/CSS migration uplifts ARM's effective rate by 2x-4x; RISC-V displacement and Qualcomm legal precedent are the caps. Whether the blended rate doubles from FY2026 to FY2030 (base) or only rises 50% (bear) is the crux.
